items.headline: Markets Closed for Weekend, AI Rally Drives Strong Week Ahead of Potential Mideast Ceasefire
items.summary: U.S. equity markets are closed today, May 31, 2026, for the weekend. The last trading day, Friday, May 29, concluded with major indices reaching new all-time highs, propelled by sustained momentum in artificial intelligence stocks and growing optimism surrounding a potential ceasefire deal in the Middle East. The S&P 500 marked its ninth consecutive week of gains.

items.statContext: Closed +0.2% on Friday, marking its ninth straight weekly gain and seventh consecutive winning day.
Closed +0.7% on Friday, surpassing 51,000 for the first time ever.
Closed +0.2% on Friday, completing an 8% increase for the month of May.
Closed -0.6% on Friday, declining from record highs but still up 1.7% for the week.

items.headline: Dell Technologies Surges on AI Demand
items.summary: Shares of Dell Technologies ($DELL) jumped nearly 30% on Friday, fueled by strong earnings that exceeded expectations and an optimistic outlook driven by robust demand for AI computing. The stock recorded a 32.8% gain for the abbreviated trading week.

items.headline: Snowflake Rallies Post-Earnings
items.summary: Cloud data company Snowflake ($SNOW) saw its stock rise by over 40% during the abbreviated trading week following its recent earnings report.

items.headline: Micron Technology Hits Trillion-Dollar Cap
items.summary: Computer memory manufacturer Micron Technology ($MU) pushed past a $1 trillion market capitalization, driven by the structural tailwind of artificial intelligence and tight supply in the memory market.

items.headline: Semiconductor Firms Beat Expectations
items.summary: Semiconductor manufacturer Marvell ($MRVL) and designer Synopsys ($SNPS) both surpassed expectations and raised their guidance after their first-quarter earnings reports.

items.headline: Inflation Remains Elevated in April, PCE Data Shows
items.summary: Both headline and core Personal Consumption Expenditure (PCE) inflation remained elevated in April. Headline PCE registered 3.8% for the month, while core PCE ticked up to 3.3%, suggesting broadening inflationary pressures beyond just energy costs.

items.headline: Kevin Warsh Sworn In as New Fed Chair
items.summary: Kevin Warsh was sworn in on Friday as the 17th Chair of the U.S. Federal Reserve. Warsh is anticipated to implement significant changes to the Fed's balance sheet as a policy tool and to revamp the central bank's communication strategy.

items.headline: Middle East Ceasefire Deal Nears, Boosting Market Sentiment
items.summary: Momentum has built this week regarding a potential ceasefire deal in the Middle East, with reports indicating the U.S. and Iran are close to an agreement. This geopolitical development has contributed to positive market sentiment.

items.headline: Gap Inc. Reports Strong Q1, Raises Outlook
items.summary: Gap Inc. ($GAP) reported solid first-quarter fiscal 2026 results on May 28, with net sales up 1% and comparable sales increasing by 2%. The company also raised its full-year earnings per share outlook.
